definition: The anti-carry/long gamma consensus trade may be approaching the point of self-defeat. If everyone owns gamma and no one owns carry, gamma becomes expensive and carry becomes cheap. The carry Sharpe confidence interval of [0.04, 0.72] shows the point estimate of 0.30-0.38 is far less precise than it appears.
evidence: Compressed spring evidence (n=18, p<0.001) underlying gamma trade is hard to dismiss
Second-order concern: universal gamma ownership makes gamma expensive and carry cheap
Carry Sharpe CI [0.04, 0.72] around point estimate 0.30-0.38
Not yet corroborated by other agents
open_questions: What positioning data would confirm gamma crowding?
At what point does gamma pricing become prohibitive relative to expected vol repricing?
Could carry become tactically attractive precisely because of anti-carry consensus?
